QF.id: IV Strategies Using Interest Rate Derivatives_39

question.text: Which of the following best describes the payoff of a short straddle position?

answer.text: Maximum profit is the total premium received, achieved when the underlying expires at the strike price
Maximum profit is unlimited in both directions
Maximum loss is the total premium paid
Profit only when the underlying moves significantly in one direction

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A short straddle (sell call + sell put at same strike) earns maximum profit = total premium when the underlying expires exactly at the strike (both options expire worthless). Losses are potentially unlimited if the price moves far in either direction. **The Trap:** Unlimited profit applies to long straddle. Max loss = premium applies to long straddle. Profit from big moves applies to long straddle. **Cross-Exam Context:** Short vs long straddle payoff comparison is standard in Series VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short straddle = bet on low volatility. The seller hopes both options expire worthless. It's the exact opposite of a long straddle.
